hero.headline: WB Government Announces Revamp of Kanyashree Scheme
hero.subheadline: To Enhance Benefits for Girl Child Education
hero.summary: The West Bengal government has announced a comprehensive revamp of its Kanyashree scheme to improve benefits for girl child education in the state. The revamped scheme aims to increase the stipend for girl students, enhance the age limit for availing benefits, and introduce new incentives for higher education. The move is expected to benefit over 1.5 million girl students in the state. According to various reports, the new scheme is set to be rolled out within the next six months.
hero.impact: To improve education prospects for girl children in West Bengal
hero.unknowns: Exact benefits and timeline of the revamped scheme

stories.headline: Farmer Protests in Bardhaman Over Land Acquisition
stories.summary: Protests broke out in Bardhaman district yesterday as local farmers resisted land acquisition by the state government for a proposed infrastructure project. The farmers claimed that they were not provided with adequate compensation and that the project would displace them from their ancestral land. The situation is still tense, with the police maintaining a heavy presence in the area.

stories.headline: Rural Health Centres in WB to Get Digital Upgrades
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to upgrade rural health centres in the state with digital infrastructure. The move aims to improve healthcare services in rural areas and reduce the gap in medical facilities. According to reports, the digital upgrades will include the implementation of electronic health records and telemedicine facilities.

stories.headline: WB Government to Launch Skill Development Programme for Youth
stories.summary: The West Bengal government is set to launch a skill development programme for youth in the state. The programme aims to equip young people with vocational skills and make them employable. According to reports, the programme will be launched in collaboration with industry partners and will focus on sectors such as IT and manufacturing.

stories.headline: West Bengal Logs Highest Number of Dengue Cases in Country
stories.summary: According to latest data, West Bengal has logged the highest number of dengue cases in the country. The state government has attributed the surge in cases to heavy rainfall and flooding in various parts of the state. Health officials have urged citizens to take precautions and prevent mosquito breeding.

stories.topic: Housing Scheme for the Poor in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: ABP Ananda: Mamata Banerjee's Government Launches Affordable Housing Scheme
state_narrative.summary: According to ABP Ananda, the West Bengal government has initiated a housing scheme to provide affordable houses to the poor. The scheme aims to construct 1 lakh houses in the next 5 years.
claims_made: 1 lakh houses will be constructed in 5 years

verified_record.headline: FactChecker.in: West Bengal's Housing Scheme for the Poor - A Fact-Check
verified_record.summary: According to FactChecker.in, the West Bengal government's housing scheme aims to construct 1 lakh houses in 5 years, but the actual number of houses constructed is yet to be seen. FactChecker.in analyzed data from the West Bengal Housing Infrastructure Development Corporation (WBHDCL) and found that the state has only constructed 0.3 lakh houses in the last 5 years.
what_was_omitted: the actual number of houses constructed is yet to be seen
verified_record.data_check: Data from the West Bengal Housing Infrastructure Development Corporation (WBHDCL) shows that the state has constructed 0.3 lakh houses in the last 5 years.

gap_analysis.key_gap: ABP Ananda did not provide context on the actual number of houses constructed under the scheme.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The actual number of houses constructed under the scheme is yet to be seen.

stories.topic: CM's Educational Initiatives in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: News18 Bangla: Mamata Banerjee Launches New Education Policy
state_narrative.summary: According to News18 Bangla, the West Bengal government has launched a new education policy aimed at improving the quality of education in the state. The policy includes introducing Bengali as a compulsory subject from class 1.
claims_made: Bengali will be introduced as a compulsory subject from class 1

verified_record.headline: Article 14: Fact-Checking the West Bengal Education Policy
verified_record.summary: According to Article 14, while the West Bengal government's education policy aims to improve the quality of education, it does not clearly mention introducing Bengali as a compulsory subject from class 1. Article 14 analyzed the policy document and found that the policy focuses on improving infrastructure and teacher training.
what_was_omitted: the policy's focus on improving infrastructure and teacher training
verified_record.data_check: The West Bengal Education Policy document does not mention introducing Bengali as a compulsory subject from class 1.

gap_analysis.key_gap: News18 Bangla's report inaccurately stated that Bengali will be introduced as a compulsory subject from class 1.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The policy focuses on improving infrastructure and teacher training.

state_narrative.headline: Zee News: Rural Poverty in West Bengal Declines
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News, rural poverty in West Bengal has declined significantly in the last 5 years. The poverty rate has decreased from 35% to 25%.
claims_made: rural poverty has declined from 35% to 25%

verified_record.headline: India Spend: Fact-Checking Rural Poverty in West Bengal
verified_record.summary: According to India Spend, while the poverty rate in West Bengal has declined, the actual numbers are not as clear-cut as Zee News reported. India Spend analyzed data from the National Family Health Survey (NFHS) and found that the poverty rate in West Bengal has declined, but the exact numbers are difficult to determine due to the complexity of the data.
what_was_omitted: the complexity of the data and the difficulty in determining exact numbers
verified_record.data_check: Data from the National Family Health Survey (NFHS) shows that the poverty rate in West Bengal has declined, but the exact numbers are difficult to determine.

gap_analysis.key_gap: Zee News did not provide context on the complexity of the data and the difficulty in determining exact numbers.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The actual numbers are difficult to determine due to the complexity of the data.

hottake.headline: Food Prices in Bengal Skyrocket: 25% Rise in June
bullets: Food prices see 25% increase in June compared to last year.
According to a report by the Centre for Monitoring Indian Economy (CMIE), the Consumer Food Price Index (CFPI) rose by 2.76% in June compared to the previous month.
This means an additional ₹500- ₹1000 spent on groceries for a family of four per month.

hottake.headline: Bengal Workers' Wage Reduce: 12% Cut in Minimum Wages
bullets: Bengal government reduces minimum wage by 12% effective July 1, 2026.
The new minimum wage stands at ₹227 per day for skilled workers and ₹187 per day for unskilled workers.
This is expected to affect over 3 million workers in the state.

hottake.headline: Bengal Power Grid on Brink: 30% Capacity Reduced
bullets: Bengal's power grid faces 30% capacity reduction due to equipment failure.
The reduction in power supply is expected to affect over 5 million households.
The state government has assured immediate repairs to restore power supply.

hottake.headline: Malaria Outbreak Spreads: 500 New Cases in Bengal
bullets: 500 new cases of malaria reported in Bengal in the past fortnight.
The outbreak is concentrated in rural areas, particularly in the South 24 Parganas district.
Health officials have attributed the rise in cases to heavy rainfall and waterlogging.

hottake.headline: Bengal's Water Crisis Deepens: 20% Reduction in Supply
bullets: Bengal's water supply decreases by 20% due to drought and water diversion.
The reduction in water supply affects over 2 million households in rural areas.
The state government has announced emergency measures to conserve water.

hottake.headline: Bengal Schools Reopen Amid Concerns: 30% Teacher Shortage
bullets: 30% shortage of teachers in Bengal schools sparks concerns among parents.
The shortage is most pronounced in rural areas, where 40% of schools lack a qualified headmaster.
The state government has assured to fill vacant positions by the end of the year.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
